title: sorbus umbellata subsp. meridionalis
intro: Sorbus umbellata subsp. meridionalis, also known as the southern mountain ash, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming the plant may provide health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties, while also potentially protecting against oxidative stress and promoting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Sorbus umbellata subsp. meridionalis can be made by infusing the bark, leaves, or roots in water or other solvents to create teas, tinctures, or decoctions for medicinal use.
